title: To Dr. Thomas Shearer.
content:
Presenting a portrait-bust of the author.



    Since you, rare friend! have tied my living tongue
    With thanks more large than man e'er said or sung,
    So let the dumbness of this image be
    My eloquence, and still interpret me.


    Baltimore, 1880.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Sidney Lanier delivers a powerful performance in "To Dr. Thomas Shearer."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
